description: §41.13 parseVariant (b) happy path — a valid tagged-variant JSON string is boundary-parsed into a PAYLOAD variant. Clicking #decode parses '{"tag":"Success","rows":42}' against LoadResult; @result becomes the typed Success(rows) value and the <Success(rows)> match arm renders "Loaded 42 rows". The cell snapshots as the author-visible payload shape { variant, data } (README (b) rule). The exhaustive !{} handler stays silent (parseError = none), proving the success path never routes through the ParseError arms.
rationale: §41.13: parseVariant(json, EnumType) lifts untyped JSON into a typed enum value via type-driven dispatch on the `tag` discriminator (the enum's own variant names). A discriminator matching a payload variant constructs that variant with its validated payload — the returned value is typed as EnumType. The payload-bearing variant snapshots as { variant, data } per the README author-visible representation.
